common.metaDescription: Measure your brand's visibility inside AI engine answers

newMeasurement.title: New measurement
newMeasurement.intro: Define the brand and your customers' questions. We run the queries and score the result.
newMeasurement.domainHint: No https://. Used to detect your linked citations.
newMeasurement.aliasesHint: Other ways the brand is written, comma-separated.
newMeasurement.competitorsHint: One per line. Used to compute your relative position.
newMeasurement.promptsHint: One per line. The questions as a customer would ask them.
newMeasurement.runsHint: How many times each prompt repeats, to measure variance.
newMeasurement.enginesHint: Pick which AIs to measure. You can choose several.
newMeasurement.mockHint: Runs the selected engines in simulated mode, spending no credits.
newMeasurement.realWarning: Real engines make paid calls; measuring several at once multiplies cost and time.
newMeasurement.errorRequired: Check the fields: a brand and at least one prompt are required.
newMeasurement.errorEngine: The measurement could not be completed: {detail}

measurements.title: Measurements
measurements.empty: No measurements saved yet. Run the first one from the terminal:

metrics.linkedCitationHint: runs with your domain among the sources

runDetail.legend: ● mention with citation · ○ mention · × no mention
runDetail.sourcesEmpty: The engine returned no sources for this measurement.

onpage.citationBotBlocked: Your robots.txt blocks {bot}: that's why you never show up on {surface}. Removing that line is the first thing to do; without it, no content work will help.
onpage.robotsServerError: Your robots.txt returns a {status} error. The protocol requires bots to assume a full block on a server error: while it stays like this, you are invisible to every AI. Fix that endpoint.
onpage.groundingBotBlocked: You block {bot}, so you lose {surface} grounding (you stay in AI Overviews, which run on Googlebot). If you want to appear on {surface}, remove that line.
onpage.trainingBotsBlocked: You block training bots ({bots}). That does not affect being cited: it's a legitimate choice about your content.
onpage.citationBotsAllowed: No citation bot is blocked in your robots.txt.
onpage.robotsMissing: You have no robots.txt, so nothing stops the AIs from reading you.
onpage.clientRendered: Your site renders content with JavaScript: the HTML carries only {chars} characters of text. ChatGPT, Claude and Perplexity don't execute JavaScript, so to them your site is empty. Render the content on the server.
onpage.rawHtmlOk: Your content travels in the HTML ({chars} characters): crawlers read it without executing JavaScript.
onpage.brandMissingInHtml: «{brand}» doesn't appear in your homepage HTML. If the name is only painted by JavaScript, the engine can't associate the page with you.
onpage.noDate: Your homepage declares no date. Content cited by AIs tends to be fresher than organic: publish with real dates and keep them current.
onpage.staleContent: Your most recent content is {days} days old. Update it with real changes; touching only the date does nothing.
onpage.dateOk: Your content declares a date {days} days old.
onpage.noTitle: Your homepage has no <title>. It's the first signal of what you are.
onpage.noDescription: Your homepage is missing its meta description.
onpage.noCanonical: No canonical tag: useful to avoid duplicate content.
onpage.invalidJsonLd: You have JSON-LD structured data that doesn't parse. Broken, it serves neither Bing nor rich results.
onpage.noSitemap: Your robots.txt declares no sitemap. Bots use it to discover pages.

recommendations.noDomain: Add your brand's domain in the settings to measure when the AIs link your site, not just when they name you.
recommendations.promptGap: You show up only {presence} of the time for «{prompt}». Publish a page that answers that question self-containedly (direct answer up top, concrete data, no fluff): the AIs recommend whoever appears in content that answers that exact question.
recommendations.linkedCitationLow: On {engine} they name you but link your site only {citation} of the time. {engine} cites sources that answer the question clearly and with data; create or improve an answer-ready page on your site about those prompts and link it to move from mention to cited source.
recommendations.engineDivergence: Your visibility varies by engine: {best} on {bestEngine} versus {worst} on {worstEngine}. Focus on {worstEngine}: check the sources section for the pages it cites for these prompts and work to be in them or to offer something better.
recommendations.absentSource: The engine cites {domain} in {frequency} of the runs and leaves you out. Get a presence there (a review, a comparison, a collaboration or a mention) or create your own content that covers the same better: it's a direct path to being cited.
recommendations.positionLow: When you do appear, you tend to trail other brands. Strengthen the signals that make you stand out: third-party reviews, concrete data (origins, prices, awards) and appearing in the sources the engine already cites.
